data.title: As euphoria ebbs, Powell on deck
data.description:
A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Rae Wee

The euphoria from this week's mix of market tailwinds appears to have run its course, with investors left searching for the next catalyst. That could come later in the day from U.S. retail sales and Walmart's earnings.

Asian stocks drifted and futures pointed to a mixed opening in Europe on Thursday, as investors sobered up to the fact that there remains much uncertainty over the outlook for the global economy and U.S. President Donald Trump's chaotic trade policies.

Key for markets will be results from Walmart , a bellwether for the U.S. retail industry, for an indication on whether the Arkansas behemoth is truly best placed to navigate the uncertainty from Trump's tariffs.

Walmart is among a handful of large companies that have not either pulled or slashed their forecasts. The company last month reaffirmed its annual forecast, saying "nothing in the current environment changes its strategy".

Should that change, investors will no doubt be alarmed.

The results come alongside U.S. retail sales data for the month of April, where expectations are for a flat reading on a monthly basis.

Together, the releases will offer clues on the health of the U.S. consumer, and upbeat numbers could narrow the chances of a recession in the world's largest economy, which would in turn be a boon for stocks.

Also on the market's radar is a speech from Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, where the focus will be on any hints he may give regarding the outlook for U.S. monetary policy.

So far, the hard data has given Fed policymakers little to go on as they try to assess how Trump's sweeping tariffs and ongoing trade negotiations will affect prices and the economy. And it is likely Powell will reiterate his patient approach on rates.

Ahead of the U.S. releases, preliminary first-quarter growth figures for the UK and euro zone are also due, though investors are likely to look past those numbers given they pre-date Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs.

In currency markets, the dollar remained volatile against the Korean won , sliding for a second straight day on news that South Korea's deputy finance minister discussed forex with a U.S. Treasury official earlier this month.

The moves in the won were reminiscent of an unprecedented surge in the Taiwan dollar just a few days ago, again stoking speculation that a weaker dollar could be part of Trump's trade deal with other countries.

Key developments that could influence markets on Thursday:

- UK preliminary GDP (Q1)

- Euro zone flash GDP (Q1)

- U.S. retail sales (April)

- Walmart earnings

- Fed Chair Powell speaks

Trying to keep up with the latest tariff news?

Our new daily news digest offers a rundown of the top market-moving headlines impacting global trade. Sign up for Tariff Watch here.

data.title: India's eClerx Services jumps after Q4 profit rise
data.description:
** Shares of eClerx Services rise 11.8% to 3,073 rupees

** Co's Q4 consol profit rose 16.6% Y/Y to 1.52 bln rupees ($17.8 mln)

** Q4 revenue up 17.2% Y/Y, while expenses jumped 19.9%

** Trading vols at 283,064 shares, more than 6x the 30-day average

** Stock set to gain for fourth straight session

** ECLE down ~12% YTD

($1 = 85.5950 Indian rupees)

data.title: CR Sanjiu Partners with CR Power on Renewable Energy Project
data.description:
China Resources Sanjiu Medical & Pharmaceutical is collaborating with affiliate CR Power to integrate traditional Chinese medicine cultivation with renewable energy projects.

Under the agreement, CR Sanjiu will provide land remediation services, including planting medicinal herbs, for CR Power's solar farms, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Annual transactions are capped at 200 million yuan, totaling up to 600 million yuan from 2025 to 2027.

data.title: African Markets - Factors to watch on May 15
data.description:
The following company announcements, scheduled economic indicators, debt and currency market moves and political events may affect African markets on Thursday.

- - - - -

EVENTS:

*Rwanda's central bank to announce its latest benchmark lending rate decision.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Stocks struggled for direction on Thursday while the dollar stumbled as the euphoria from market tailwinds earlier in the week fizzled out, with traders looking to U.S. data later in the day for further catalysts.

WORLD OIL PRICES

Oil prices lost nearly $1 in early trade on Thursday on expectations of a potential U.S.-Iran nuclear deal, while an unexpected build in U.S. crude oil inventories last week heightened investor concerns about oversupply.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

AFRICA STOCKS

For the latest news on South African stocks, click on

SOUTH AFRICA MARKETS

South Africa's rand recouped some losses on Wednesday, a day after the country's power utility implemented rolling power cuts.

NIGERIA PETROLEUM

Nigeria is discussing exploration of its deep water oil acreage with Petrobras, years after the Brazilian oil giant exited the west African nation, Nigeria's Foreign Minister Yusuf Tuggar said.

KENYA MARKETS

The Kenyan shilling was unchanged against the dollar on Wednesday, data from the London Stock Exchange Group showed.

ETHIOPIA INVESTMENT

Ethiopia has signed investment deals for its minerals and energy sectors worth more than $1.7 billion, mostly with Chinese firms, its finance ministry said.

MALI BARRICK MINING

A court in Mali will hold a hearing on Thursday to consider a request made by Mali's military government to reopen Canadian miner Barrick's Loulo-Gounkoto gold mine under a provisional administration, a court official said.

data.title: Huagong Tech to Establish Suzhou Lihua Technology JV
data.description:
Huagong Tech subsidiary HGTECH Industry will establish a joint venture with Likai Precision in Suzhou to develop 3D SLM additive manufacturing.

Suzhou Lihua Technology will have a registered capital of 200 million yuan, with Huagong Tech holding 70% and Likai Precision holding 30%, according to a .

The joint venture aims to drive innovation in sectors such as consumer electronics, automotive and healthcare.

data.title: Fitch Affirms SBB's Rating on Weak Capital Structure
data.description:
Fitch affirmed on Wednesday the CCC long-term issuer default rating of SBB - Samhällsbyggnadsbolaget i Norden AB (SBB-B.ST, SBB-D.ST), d/b/a SBB, citing the group's weakened capital structure.

The rating agency attributed the weakness to transferring most of the group's assets and debt to its subsidiary, Samhällsbyggnadsbolaget i Norden Holding AB.

Fitch noted that the remaining assets of the parent company are not enough to support 17 billion kronor of retained debt, including hybrids.

Geely Automobile's Profit More Than Tripled on Record Sales
data.description:
(04:03 GMT) *Geely Automobile 1Q Rev CNY72.50B Vs. CNY58.23B >0175.HK
(04:04 GMT) *Geely Automobile 1Q Net CNY5.67B Vs. Net CNY1.56B >0175.HK
(04:04 GMT) *Geely Automobile Achieved Record Sales in 1Q >0175.HK
(04:06 GMT) *Geely Automobile 1Q Vehicle Sales of Galaxy Brand More Than Tripled on Year >0175.HK
(04:06 GMT) *Geely Automobile Witnessed Significant Scale Effects, Substantial Increase in Overall Profitability >0175.HK
(04:24 GMT) Geely Automobile's Profit More Than Tripled on Record Sales

By Jiahui Huang

Geely Automobile's first-quarter net profit more than tripled, thanks to record sales and improvement in profitability, as it strives to make headway in China's increasingly competitive electric-vehicle market.

The company said Thursday that net profit more than tripled to 5.67 billion yuan, equivalent to $786.6 million. Revenue for the quarter rose to 72.50 billion yuan from 58.23 billion yuan a year earlier.

The Chinese automaker said it achieved record sales in the first quarter and saw robust growth in the new-energy business. Its total sales volume jumped 48% to 703.8 million units during the period. Sales of its Galaxy brand more than tripled, with improved profitability for the new-energy segment, Geely added.

Robust sales have led to significant scale effects and a substantial increase in overall profitability, the carmaker said.

Geely is China's second-largest EV maker and has been expanding its market share with strong product offerings.

The company has reorganized some of its resources over the past year. Last week, Geely said it is considering taking its premium EV brand Zeekr private, just one year after it was listed in the U.S.

Zeekr's potential privatization will likely support Geely's synergies with regard to research and development, supply chain and product portfolio concentration, HSBC Global Research analysts wrote in a note.

Two of Geely's brands--Zeekr and Lynk & Co--also recently merged to form Zeekr Group in order to maximize the group's resources.

Geely's Hong Kong-listed shares have risen 32% this year, outperforming the benchmark Hang Seng Index's 18% rise.

Write to Jiahui Huang at jiahui.huang@wsj.com

data.title: Nexi Completes EUR750 Million Senior Unsecured Notes Issuance
data.description:
Nexi (NEXI.MI, NEXI.VI) completed the placement of 750 million euros of 3.875% fixed-rate senior unsecured non-convertible notes due May 21, 2031.

The issuance is the first under the payment technology group's non-convertible euro medium-term note program, totaling 4 billion euros, according to a Wednesday release.

The notes, which have an issue price of 99.89%, are scheduled to be settled on May 21.

data.title: Saudi Regulator OKs Financial Securities and Brokerage Company's New Fund
data.description: The Saudi Capital Market Authority on Wednesday approved Alistithmar for Financial Securities and Brokerage Company's public offer for the Alistithmar Capital Parallel Market Companies Fund (Nomu).

data.title: Australian Pacific Coal Secures Additional Funding for Dartbrook Project
data.description:
Australian Pacific Coal said its Dartbrook joint venture with Tetra Resources has entered into a further amendment of the existing senior secured facility with Vitol Asia to increase the commitment by $4.6 million to $108.6 million from $104 million, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The company will use the facility for working capital to cover the short-term funding needs of the Dartbrook project, due to anticipated washed coal sales.

The terms of the facility are consistent with the previous senior refinancing, the filing added.

data.title: Turkey - Factors To Watch on May 15
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Thursday.

The lira was quoted at 38.77 against the U.S. dollar at 0400 GMT, unchanged from its close on Thursday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed slightly higher at 9,701.55 points on Thursday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Stocks struggled for direction on Thursday while the dollar stumbled as the euphoria from market tailwinds earlier in the week fizzled out, with traders looking to U.S. data later in the day for further catalysts.

ERDOGAN SCHEDULE

President Tayyip Erdogan will welcome Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy in Ankara and hold bilateral talks (1000 GMT). Erdogan will then attend an event of his Ak Party and business group later in the evening. Erdogan's schedule does not include him meeting the Russian and Ukrainian delegations for meeting in Istanbul.

NATO FM MEETING

NATO foreign ministers arrive for informal meeting and make doorstep remarks to media. NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte holds news conference following the meeting of NATO foreign ministers.

FIDAN NEWS CONFERENCE

Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan will hold a press conference following an informal meeting of NATO foreign ministers (1100 GMT). Fidan is scheduled to meet with the Germany, France, U.S. and Syrian counterparts.

RUSSIA-UKRAINE TALKS

Russia and Ukraine are set to hold their first direct, high-level talks in Istanbul in more than three years of war, seeking a possible ceasefire to the deadliest conflict in Europe since World War Two.

TRUMP-PUTIN

U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin indicated they would not attend what could be the first direct peace talks between Moscow and Kyiv in three years on Thursday, with the Kremlin sending instead a group of experienced technocrats.

VICE PRESIDENT

Turkey's Vice President Cevdet Yilmaz visits Uzbekistan, attends business and economy events.

CENTRAL BANK

Turkish central bank will announce statistics on net, gross reserves and data on stock, bond holding of investors (1130 GMT).

BUDGET BALANCE

The Ministry of Treasury and Finance will publish the April budget balance data. (0800 GMT)

data.title: SPPC's Board Proposes Capital Structure Optimization
data.description:
Saudi Printing and Packaging Co.'s (SASE:4270), or SPPC, board proposed to optimize the company's capital structure at an extraordinary general assembly held Wednesday, according to a same-day release.

The proposal includes filing a request to the Capital Market Authority for an increase in the Saudi Arabian paper and plastics manufacturer's capital through an issue of 5,207,064 shares to Alinma Bank (SASE:1150) for a total issuance value of 73.7 million Saudi riyals. EFG Hermes Saudi Arabia will act as the financial adviser for the process.

In addition, the board proposed a capital reduction following the completion of the increase to bring accumulated losses below 50% of its capital.

The recommendations are subject to approval from regulatory authorities and the extraordinary general assembly.

data.title: Man Wah: Will Raise R&D Investment to Launch More Smart, Green and Environmentally Friendly Products >1999.HK
Toppan Holdings' Fiscal Full-Year Profit Jumps 20%
data.description:
Toppan Holdings' profit attributable to owners of the parent jumped 20% to 89.3 billion yen for the fiscal full-year ended March 31, from 74.2 billion yen a year ago.

The printing company's basic earnings per share climbed to 295.98 yen from 230.96 yen a year ago, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on Wednesday.

Revenue rose 2.4% to 1.72 trillion yen from 1.68 trillion yen a year earlier.

The company declared a year-end dividend of 32.00 per share, bringing the full-year dividend to 56.00 per share. Payments will commence on June 30.

For the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026, the company expects attributable profit of 65.0 billion yen, basic EPS of 230.40 yen per share, while revenue is projected to reach 1.880 trillion yen.

The company plans to pay a total dividend of 56.00 yen per share for the current fiscal year.

data.title: VEON 1Q Ebitda $439M >VEON
BOJ expected to pause rate hikes through September amid tariffs uncertainty - poll
data.description:
• 59 of 62 economists (95%) see no change to interest rates in June meeting
  • 39 of 58 economists (67%) see interest rates staying unchanged in Q3 (previously 36%)
  • 30 of 58 economists (52%) see at least one more rate hike by year-end
  • The median prediction for end-September rate is now 0.50% (previously 0.75%)
  • The median prediction for year-end rate is 0.75% (unchanged)
  • 16 of 29 economists (55%) either strongly or somewhat approve of the Japanese government's approach to tariff negotiations with the US

data.title: Australia's Xero rises on higher full-year profit
data.description:
Shares of Australia's Xero rose to its strongest level in nearly three months on Thursday after the accounting software provider reported a 30% rise in annual net profit.

The New Zealand-based firm posted a net profit after tax of NZ$227.8 million ($134.5 million) for fiscal year 2025, up from NZ$174.6 million a year earlier.

($1 = 1.6938 New Zealand dollars)

data.title: Wendt India set for worst day in 17 years on parent's stake sale at discount
data.description:
** Wendt India plunges 17% to 8,689 rupees; set for steepest intraday pct drop since January 2008

** Parent Wendt Gmbh to sell 37.5% stake in WNDT on May 15 and May 16 at floor price of 6,500 rupees, a 38% discount to Wednesday's close

** Stock on track to snap three-session gaining streak

** WNDT extends YTD losses to 48%
